claim: FailureOps finds paired intervention-sensitive logical failure behavior on real QEC detector records.
why_it_matters: Treats logical failure as an intervention-sensitive system event on public shot-level records.
scope_boundary: Evidence uses one public Google RL QEC dataset family rather than independent hardware campaigns.

claim: Pairing is methodologically necessary because it reduces estimator variance and exposes rescued/induced transitions.
key_metric: mean unpaired/paired bootstrap std ratio on original and expanded corpora
why_it_matters: Shows that pairing is a measurement requirement, not just an implementation detail.
scope_boundary: Bootstrap does not replace external replication.

claim: Static burden and plain LFR baselines do not provide intervention attribution.
key_metric: baseline-vs-FailureOps ranking agreement plus missing rescued/induced semantics
why_it_matters: Separates ranking difficult conditions from attributing which controllable factor changed failure behavior.
scope_boundary: Ranking agreement can be high because difficult conditions also have larger intervention effects.

claim: Measured decoder-runtime replay can be closed into paired runtime deadline interventions.
why_it_matters: Connects measured decoder service time to a runtime intervention that changes logical outcomes on the same shots.
scope_boundary: Replay measures local decoder service time, not live control-stack queueing or hardware feedback latency.

claim: The real-record decoder-pathway sensitivity result externally replicates on the qec3v5 public surface-code scaling dataset.
why_it_matters: Shows that the main paired decoder-pathway result is not confined to one Google RL QEC release.
scope_boundary: qec3v5 is a separate public experiment dataset but still from Google.

claim: The paired logical-failure sensitivity signal remains broadly net-rescuing on the broader Google RL QEC v2 same-family corpus, including both observed control modes and both logical bases.
key_metric: net-rescuing fraction, subgroup means, and scope-wise Holm count on the v2 corpus
why_it_matters: Strengthens the story from a focused public matrix to a broader same-family real-record corpus without changing the thesis.
scope_boundary: The v2 expansion broadens the Google public corpus but is not an independent non-Google replication.

claim: FailureOps scales from a focused prior sweep to a corpus-level prior intervention study on public detector records.
key_metric: bootstrap-CI support rate plus mean paired delta by prior family
why_it_matters: Turns decoder-prior analysis into a systems-style intervention family rather than a single anecdotal condition.
scope_boundary: The current prior corpus is same-family Google public evidence with a fixed decoder backend, not an independent cross-lab prior replication.
